code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test13-AUG-2015Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, bilirubinuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance and color, supporting renal dysfunction and possible tubular damage. Hemoglobin A1c is low, which may reflect altered red cell turnover or analytical interference. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended.
